# Little Women

Louisa May Alcott's novel invites attention to family life, moral growth, work, friendship, and the shaping of character.

## Spelling Words

| Core Words | Challenge Words |
|---|---|
| sister | character |
| family | sacrifice |
| mother | generous |
| hearth | responsibility |
| letter | affection |
| dress | ambition |
| music | contentment |
| work | forgiveness |
| kind | perseverance |
| home | conscience |

## Word Study

- Group words into home, work, virtue, and feeling.
- Choose three virtue words and write an example from the story for each.
- Practice spelling words with suffixes: kindness, generous, forgiveness, perseverance.

## Narration

- Narrate one chapter by focusing only on Jo's choices.
- Explain how one sister's weakness can also be connected to a strength.
- Describe a scene of ordinary home life and why it matters.

## Writing Prompts

- Write a letter from one sister to another after a difficult day.
- Compare ambition with duty.
- Describe a family custom that helps form character.

## Projects

- Create a March family character chart with virtues, faults, and growth.
- Plan a simple service project inspired by the novel.
- Stage a short parlor performance with narration and dialogue.
